summary.intent: Fix new PR comments on PR #636, including Codecov coverage gaps in crates/oauth/src/redirect.rs by removing unreachable defensive branches.
summary.outcome: Simplified the redirect normalization function to eliminate dead code branches, all 240 tests pass across 4 crates, committed and pushed with Greptile comment replied.

repo: The moltis repo uses Greptile for automated PR code review that can open new issues post-merge.
Test suite is split across moltis-mcp (77), moltis-oauth (61), moltis-oauth-integration (9), and moltis-provider-setup (93) crates.
Parallel test execution makes process-global state (std::env::set_var) unsafe for integration tests — pure unit tests are preferred.

code.finding: host_str() returns IPv6 addresses with brackets (e.g. [::1]) which breaks IpAddr::from_str; must use .host() matching instead. Using is_some_and avoids a dedicated None match arm.
The None => false branch and set_scheme().is_err() fallback were provably unreachable: Url::parse on a special scheme always produces a host, and set_scheme from https to http always succeeds.
normalize_loaded_redirect_uri(&mut OAuthConfig) is the extracted pure helper for redirect URI normalization from config files.

workflow: Codecov flags uncovered lines as PR comments, requiring defensive/unreachable branches to be removed rather than tested.
Running format + clippy before committing is standard practice in this repo.
Greptile comments should be replied to directly on GitHub after addressing the underlying issue.

friction: Initial simplification of redirect.rs broke IPv6 tests because host_str() bracket behavior wasn't anticipated, requiring a second edit pass.
Unreachable defensive branches in safe Rust code create Codecov noise that must be resolved by code deletion rather than test coverage.
